All right, so like I said in the last video, the purpose of our work together this week is to move on to level two campaigns, which is essentially the campaigns we create after we build our seed campaigns. These are the campaigns we create based on the data that we got from our first round of testing. All right, so what we're going to be doing for the rest of our time together during all of these sessions is just really showing you how to scale these level two campaigns. And there are campaigns beyond level two, incidentally. But you always want to make sure you sort of follow this framework that I'm giving you because it increases your likelihood of making money, generally increases the speed at which you make money and it also minimizes your risk. All right, so the first thing I want to do is I want to walk you through how to read this stuff on Facebook. I'm not going to spend a lot of time on this because maybe you already know how to do this. All right, so let's go to the computer. And when we are here, we'll see that I'm in my Facebook account and the campaigns we're going to be paying attention to are these, all right? These are the ones that we built together, okay? During this class. Now I've also built some of these other campaigns prior to the class and like, I didn't walk you through building them and some of them are screwed up, so we'll fix them. And here as well, but we're going to start with these two. So the first thing I want you to notice is our reporting. All right, notice here I have this reporting, so you can change your reporting probably. If you're new to all this stuff, you'll see this type of reporting. It's literally like generally set to performance right here. And performance is okay because it shows you like how many results you got. So I'm optimizing for purchases and your reach impressions and all that stuff doesn't really matter. And then it'll show you your amount spent. It sometimes it'll show you your website purchases and sometimes it will show you your return on ad spend. But what we want to do is we want to create a report that'll show you exactly what you want to see. So what you can do is it's right over here and again, like yours may say performance or anything, but you literally just click on this menu no matter what it says, right? Not reports, but this one. So generally it says customize all the time. And click on customize columns down here. All right, and what we can do now is we can tell Facebook what we want to see, right? So what data that we want to see with all of this stuff here. And I like a very simple streamlined approach to this. So I'm gonna click over here again and I'm gonna go to customize columns and I'm gonna get rid of a lot of this stuff. All right, so delivery just means is it on or not? We'll leave that, we'll leave the ad set name, I'll leave the bid strategy, I'll leave the budget. I'm gonna get rid of this, I'm gonna get rid of attribution setting, results I want, this is how many purchases or leads or whatever. I don't really care about reach, I don't care about impressions, but I do care about cost per result, right? I don't care about quality ranking, I don't care about engagement rate ranking, I don't care about conversion rate ranking, I do care about amount spent though. I don't care about ends, I don't care about schedule, I do care about link clicks, and I do care about website purchases. On Facebook purchases I don't care about, we're not utilizing the ability to actually sell things on the Facebook platform right now. And I also care about website purchase, return on ad spend. So if we just displayed this data, we would have like really solid data here, but I wanna get a little bit more. Specifically, I wanna get some data that I can use to troubleshoot campaigns if they are funky. So one thing that I'm gonna want is I'm going to want unique, if you just type in the word unique here, I'm gonna wanna track unique outbound clicks, all right? I'm gonna move this over here to link clicks, and I'm actually going to delete link clicks. This means someone has seen the ad and they are clicking to leave Facebook and go to my website, all right? So that could be a link or it could be the button or whatever, but these are people like actually leaving, okay? I also want, all right, the cost per unique outbound click, all right? So that's going to tell me how much I'm paying per click. And you know what, I just deleted the unique outbound click thing. So I messed that up, where'd we go? Okay, where are you? Unique outbound clicks, there we go, all right. And see how you can move stuff around here? So you can organize yours however you want to. But I just kinda like to have mine organized like this so I can just sort of have a quick glance, like, all right, what'd I spend, okay? And what's the cost per click? And another thing I want is I wanna get unique click-through rate, all right? And specifically unique outbound click-through rate. So this is like the rate at which people click on my ad. Because if I'm showing my ad to a lot of people, but nobody's actually clicking, then it's the ad's fault. It doesn't mean my business is broken, it doesn't mean my website is necessarily broken. It means that my ad sucks, okay? So I'm gonna move this over here as well. I'll put that right there. Alrighty, so that's pretty good. We'll take this one and what do we have here? We have our cost per result, we have our amount spent. I'm gonna move my amount spent right over here, okay? Like that, and so it'll, the order in which they're going up and down like this is going to be the order that they're displayed left and right in here. And I think this is pretty good for now. Cause I'm just really wanting to see, all right, you know, who bought stuff. If I was generating leads, incidentally, we're opt-ins or whatever, I could type in leads and I could be like, okay, you know, show me how many leads these ads got. I'm not tracking leads through these campaigns, so I won't, but that's how you do it, all right? So I'm not gonna mess with that, okay? So I'm gonna click on save as preset. All right, and I'm gonna just type in simple demo, okay? And I'm gonna hit apply here. Now, let's go back to these campaigns we created, all right? Which are these two, okay? So one thing you gotta understand is that I'm using conversions API to track data here. So all of the purchases and stuff were being passed back through Facebook. But if we look, I'm looking at August 16th through 24th for these two campaigns. If I look at high roasts, all right? August 16th through 24th for these two campaigns, notice it's tracking the cost, okay? How much I've spent, 734, 32, 161, 40, let's see. Where are you? 734, 05, 161, 03. So there's a slight delay, because these campaigns are still running. You know, it's spent like 20 more cents or whatever that it hasn't picked up. It's not showing in Facebook yet, but it's showing in high roast. All right, so it's getting the spend right, but it's not getting our return on ad spend and our amount of purchases right in Facebook. So if we look at these, we can see that, man, you know, in total, like for this one, four course interest demo, which is the big one that we made, right? It's this guy, the one with a whole bunch of different audiences and stuff. It's saying that we're losing money here, okay? That we've only gotten 0.33% return. When in fact, we look at high roasts, we've gotten 1.51%, which is good. This is why I recommend platforms like high roasts. There is one thing to keep in mind though, and that is that A, these campaigns haven't been on very long, like a week in total, right? And B, even by Facebook's own admission, there's a delay in them getting accurate stats here. So if you don't wanna use something like high roasts or whatever, just understand that your stats aren't gonna be entirely accurate for a while. It takes a couple of days for everything to catch up. And B, even then, it doesn't necessarily display all the right data, you know? So which is like, once again, the case for something like high roasts or whatever. You could also use Redneck Analytics, which is, you know, we build one funnel that is just for Facebook traffic. One funnel that is exactly like the other one, but it's only for YouTube traffic, and then you do the measurement that way. I call that Redneck Analytics style that's pretty good. All right, so this is how we like, are able to get the data in Facebook right here. And so let's look at these guys, okay? If we take this big one, for example, we can see in Facebook here that I can sort, first of all, all right? So where are we? Look, is it in my normal? I said the wrong one, I wanna get a simple demo. You see it just switched on me out of nowhere. What are you doing, Facebook? You're messing me up. All right, so I can sort here, and I can see, okay, this HubSpot audience really crushed, you know? And Gary Vaynerchuk audience did really well, and these other audiences, man, they didn't do that well. You know, they were okay. Again, this isn't completely accurate. If I go to high roasts, and I look here, all right? I'm gonna drop down here, and if I wanna sort by a return on ad spend with these guys, it will show me something entirely different. I trust, all right, the other data, okay? More, so I'm trusting high roasts way more than I'm trusting Facebook's data, and the reason why is because it's more accurate. Like, high roasts is a gazillion dollars just to get attribution right, where at Facebook, they're kind of reactive right now, and they're not really, that's not really what they're doing. But if we look at it as it's okay, you know? So, the same HubSpot was 2.79. I trust this one, Facebook is saying, you know, HubSpot was 4.42, so actually that was way off, so never mind there, okay? So, this is an unfortunate reality. Like, this is just how it goes with Facebook stats. So, they're not entirely accurate. Choose your tracking solution wisely. I recommend high roasts, you know, but do whatever you wanna do, okay? So, with all of this said, we know what we're looking at now.
